Your task is to analyze the formal proof of this theorem in Isabelle,<br/> focusing on the subgoal at the masked location(&prime;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#b22222;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>/*@ &gt;&gt;&gt; lemma &lt;&lt;&lt; */</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&prime;). This subgoal has not been verified in Isabelle, and you must request a relevant <br/>lemma,Supplement the assumptions required to prove the subgoals, meeting the following criteria: -the lemma should guide Sledgehammer to <br/>prove the subgoal. -you should provide the formal statement of the lemma in Isabelle (without proving the lemma).<br/><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Function definitions</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>typedef </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">(overloaded) (&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue =&quot;{xs :: (&prime;a × &prime;b) list. distinct (map fst xs) ∧ sorted (map snd xs)}&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">morphisms </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">alist_of Abs_pq<br/></font><font Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> -<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> &quot;[] ∈ ?pri_queue&quot; by simp<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q = map fst (alist_of q)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> priorities :: &quot;(&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue ⇒ &prime;b list&quot; (&quot;∥(_)∥&quot;) </font><font Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q = map snd (alist_of q)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q ≡ map_of (alist_of q)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> min :: &quot;(&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue ⇒ &prime;a&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q ≡ fst (hd (alist_of q))&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">empty :: &quot;(&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">≡ Abs_pq []&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">push :: &quot;&prime;a ⇒ &prime;b::linorder ⇒ (&prime;a, &prime;b) pri_queue ⇒ (&prime;a, &prime;b) pri_queue&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">push </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">k p q ≡ Abs_pq (if k ∉ set (values q)<br/> then insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)else alist_of q)&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition</b></font><font (&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">remove_min </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q ≡ (if is_empty q <br/> then empty else Abs_pq (tl (alist_of q)))&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>definition </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">pop :: &quot;(&prime;a, &prime;b::linorder) pri_queue ⇒ (&prime;a × (&prime;a, &prime;b) pri_queue) option&quot; </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>where </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> &quot;</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">pop </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">q = (if is_empty q <br/> then None else Some (min q, remove_min q))&quot;<br/><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Theorem statement</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>theorem</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> push_values:<br/> &quot;set |push k p q| = set |q| \&lt;union&gt; {k}&quot;<br/>```<br/><br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Formal proof</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>theorem</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> push_values:<br/> &quot;set |push k p q| = set |q| \&lt;union&gt; {k}&quot;<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>proof</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> (</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">cases </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;k \&lt;notin&gt; set (values q)&quot;)<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">case </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">True<br/> (* Step1: New Queue Construction *)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">h1:&quot;push k p q = Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))&quot;	by (simp add: True push_def)<br/> (* Step2: Values of the New Queue *)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h2:&quot;values (push k p q) = map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))))&quot;	by (simp add: h1 values_def)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> then have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h3:&quot;... = map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))&quot;		</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#b22222;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> /*@ &gt;&gt;&gt; lemma &lt;&lt;&lt; */<br/></b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> (* Step3: The insort_key snd function maintains the order and includes the new value k. *)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h4:&quot;set (map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))) = set (map fst (alist_of q)) ∪ {k}&quot;		by (simp add: set_insort_key)<br/> (* Step4: which implies *)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> then </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">show</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#0099ff;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">?thesis</font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">		by (simp add: h1 h3 values_def)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>next</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/> (* Step5: Case 2: k is already in the set of values of q *)<br/> case False<br/> (* Step6: Queue Unchanged *)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">h5:&quot;push k p q = Abs_pq (alist_of q)&quot;		by (simp add: False push_def)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> then have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h6:&quot;... = q&quot;		using alist_of_inverse by auto<br/> (* Step7: Values of the Unchanged Queue *)<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>then have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h7:&quot;set (values (push k p q )) = set (values q)&quot;		by (simp add: h5)<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b> have</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> h8:&quot;set |q| ∪ {k} = set (values q)&quot;	using False by auto<br/> Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> : then have h3:&quot;map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)))) = map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))&quot;<br/>In Isabelle, this step cannot be verified by sledgehammer because the reasoning rules are either unclear or the reasoning gap is too large. <br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>## Required new lemma</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><br/>Thought1:The rule Abs_pq_inverse states that if a list xs satisfies the conditions expected by Abs_pq (i.e., sorted by priority and distinct keys),..<br/>This rule is complete in the sense that it perfectly characterizes the behavior of the Abs_pq constructor when applied to lists that meet the necessary ...<br/>Sorted by Priority ensures that the list is a valid representation of a priority queue. Distinct Keys ensures that no two pairs in the list have the same key, ...<br/>### new lemma1<br/>```isabelle<br/></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#006699;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>lemma</b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"> alist_of_Abs_pq:<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>assumes </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">a1:&quot;distinct (map fst xs)&quot;<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">a2:&quot;sorted (map snd xs)&quot;<br/> </font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#009966;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1"><b>shows </b></font><font style="font-size:10.58px;font-family:Times New Roman;color:#000000;direction:ltr;letter-spacing:0px;line-height:120%;opacity:1">&quot;alist_of (Abs_pq xs) = xs&quot;<br/>```</font></p></div>" tags="Background" id="sixPqVdEzeB0jIY1dAgv-26"> <mxCell p.font:
You are an expert in theorem proving using the Isabelle theorem prover. Your task is to analyze the formal proof of this theorem in Isabelle,
focusing on the subgoal at the masked location(′

p.font:
′). This subgoal has not been verified in Isabelle, and you must request a relevant
lemma,Supplement the assumptions required to prove the subgoals, meeting the following criteria: -the lemma should guide Sledgehammer to
prove the subgoal. -you should provide the formal statement of the lemma in Isabelle (without proving the lemma).

p.font: (overloaded) (′a, ′b::linorder) pri_queue ="{xs :: (′a × ′b) list. distinct (map fst xs) ∧ sorted (map snd xs)}"

p.font:
k p q ≡ Abs_pq (if k ∉ set (values q)
then insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)else alist_of q)"

p.font:
h1:"push k p q = Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))" by (simp add: True push_def)
(* Step2: Values of the New Queue *)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font: h2:"values (push k p q) = map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))))" by (simp add: h1 values_def)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font: h3:"... = map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))"

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(178, 34, 34), rgb(255, 155, 155)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font: (* Step3: The insort_key snd function maintains the order and includes the new value k. *)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
h4:"set (map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))) = set (map fst (alist_of q)) ∪ {k}" by (simp add: set_insort_key)
(* Step4: which implies *)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
(* Step5: Case 2: k is already in the set of values of q *)
case False
(* Step6: Queue Unchanged *)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font: h5:"push k p q = Abs_pq (alist_of q)" by (simp add: False push_def)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
h6:"... = q" using alist_of_inverse by auto
(* Step7: Values of the Unchanged Queue *)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font: h7:"set (values (push k p q )) = set (values q)" by (simp add: h5)

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 102, 153), rgb(92, 180, 223)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;
font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
h8:"set |q| ∪ {k} = set (values q)" using False by auto
(* Step9: Conclusion *)

p.font:
: then have h3:"map fst (alist_of (Abs_pq (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q)))) = map fst (insort_key snd (k, p) (alist_of q))"
In Isabelle, this step cannot be verified by sledgehammer because the reasoning rules are either unclear or the reasoning gap is too large.

font.style: font-size: 10.58px; font-family: "Times New Roman"; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(237, 237, 237)); direction: ltr; letter-spacing: 0px; line-height: 120%; opacity: 1;

p.font:
Thought1:The rule Abs_pq_inverse states that if a list xs satisfies the conditions expected by Abs_pq (i.e., sorted by priority and distinct keys),..
This rule is complete in the sense that it perfectly characterizes the behavior of the Abs_pq constructor when applied to lists that meet the necessary ...
Sorted by Priority ensures that the list is a valid representation of a priority queue. Distinct Keys ensures that no two pairs in the list have the same key, ...
